text.title: Audience to Customer Funnel Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding the Audience to Customer Funnel methodology. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Stages are exactly: Awareness, Interest, Consideration, Conversion. Custom stage names are forbidden — they kill comparability.
rule.rationale: Custom naming makes cross-channel + cross-funnel comparison impossible.

rule.statement: Each stage names exactly ONE KPI. Multiple KPIs per stage are forbidden (operator must pick the load-bearing one).
rule.rationale: Tracking five things per stage = tracking nothing; forcing one focuses intervention.

rule.statement: Each stage names a single owner. For a solo founder it's themselves; for a 2-person team it's split. 'team' / 'we' / '' forbidden.
Each stage declares a drop-off threshold (% from prior stage) that triggers intervention. Without a threshold, drops go unnoticed.
rule.rationale: Numbers without thresholds are dashboards, not actions.

rule.statement: Baseline rates per stage use ≥30 days of data. <30 days is labelled 'provisional' — interventions on provisional baselines are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Day-of-week + campaign noise drowns out signal under 30 days.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent's context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-audience-to-customer-funnel.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
